docking_tool: Various (review: ZDOCK / FireDock / HawkRank / ZRANK / dDFIRE / PBSA_E)
target_class: Various — kinases / proteases / GPCRs / PPIs / nuclear receptors
target_name: Multiple (e.g. ALK / HIV-1 protease / α-thrombin / PLK1)
virtual_screening_library_size: N/A — review covers studies using DUD database (~0.7M actives/decoys) among others
MM_GBSA_performed: Y — core topic of review; extensively discussed
MM_GBSA_delta_G_kcal_mol: Various by target (review aggregates across studies)
MM_GBSA_protocol: Multiple protocols discussed — key recommendations: GB-OBC or GB-HCT implicit solvent; variable interior dielectric (ε_in = 2 for hydrophobic pockets; higher for charged sites); interaction entropy correction improves accuracy; single-trajectory protocol standard
MD_simulation_performed: Y — reviewed as standard pre-MM/GBSA step
MD_duration_ns: Variable by cited study — review authors conclude <5 ns is reasonable; 1 ns MD in explicit solvent (AMBER ff03) confirmed critical for MM/GBSA in one benchmark
MD_force_field: AMBER ff94 / ff99 / ff03 / ff99SB / ff12SB benchmarked; ff99SB recommended for most protein-ligand systems
RMSD_stability_Å: N/A — review article; RMSD stability not aggregated
experimental_validation: Various (IC50 / Ki cited as reference data in multiple reviewed studies — e.g. 6 nM / 110 nM)
Spearman_rho_docking_vs_exp: rs=0.854 reported for FEP benchmark (one study); not primary MM-GBSA result
Spearman_rho_MMGBSA_vs_exp: rs=0.767 (PLK1 inhibitors; 8 ns MD sampling); rs=0.88–0.91 (α-thrombin; ε_in=2)
Pearson_r_reported: rp=0.66 for 98 protein-ligand complexes (MM/GBSA; ε_in=2.0) — large benchmark dataset
TF_specific: N — nuclear receptors mentioned but no TF-specific analysis
evidence_tier: 3 (Computational review — no primary data; aggregates multiple primary studies)
notes: CRITICAL MM-GBSA BENCHMARKS FOR DAM-DRUG META-ANALYSIS: Best-case rs=0.88–0.91 (α-thrombin; near-ideal conditions). Realistic large-benchmark rp=0.66 (98 complexes). TF targets likely closer to rp=0.66 than rs=0.90 due to shallower/flatter binding sites. PROTOCOL RECOMMENDATIONS DIRECTLY APPLICABLE: (1) Use ε_in=2 for hydrophobic pocket targets (likely appropriate for SPI1 ETS domain / RUNX1-CBFB interface); (2) 1–5 ns MD is sufficient — 100 ns plan is conservative and well-justified; (3) AMBER ff99SB recommended; (4) Single-trajectory protocol is standard. PLAN REVIEW RULE: Meta-analysis pooled ρ threshold = 0.50. Wang 2019 data range is rs=0.66–0.91 — well above threshold. Provides prior expectation distribution for meta-analysis. LIMITATION: No AlphaFold2 data; no TF-specific ρ; no CNS/BBB.

docking_scoring_function: Default scoring functions of each tool — combined via Exponential Consensus Ranking (ECR) and Pose and Rank Consensus (PRC)
target_class: Diverse — kinases / GPCRs / nuclear receptors / proteases / enzymes
target_name: 22 targets: ADRB2, ANDR, CDK2, COX1, ESR1, FABP4, HSP90α, IGF1R, LFA1, PRGR, KPCB, PTN1, PNPH, RENI, ABL1, UROK, DRD3, KITH, PDE5A, FA7, HXK4, PYRD
PDB_or_AF2_used: Both PDB and AlphaFold2 (direct head-to-head comparison) — PDB IDs: 4LDO/2AM9/1FVV/2OYU/3ERD/2NNQ/1UYG/2OJ9/2ICA/3KBA/2I0E/2AZR/3BGS/3G6Z/2HZI/1SQT/3PBL/2UZ3/1UDT/1W7X/3F9M/1D3G
binding_site_definition: Binding pockets defined by holo PDB ligand positions — same pocket coordinates applied to AF2 models
virtual_screening_library_size: DUD-E and NRLiSt databases — exact N not reported
hit_rate_pct: PRC Hit Rate on AF2 models = 0.16 (vs. PDB models — HR not directly stated but implied higher)
top_compound_docking_score: N/A — enrichment factor metric used; not absolute docking scores
experimental_validation: N/A — retrospective benchmark using known actives/decoys (no wet-lab IC50 measured)
AF2_pLDDT_threshold: No explicit pLDDT exclusion threshold — binding-site pLDDT scores ranged 72–98 across 22 targets; high pLDDT did NOT predict good docking performance (critical finding)
AF2_docking_RMSD_vs_crystal_Å: AF2 vs. PDB backbone RMSD: entire protein generally low; binding site: ICM native ligand docking on AF2 = 0.66–>10 Å (vs. PDB self-docking 0.15–6.3 Å) — AF2 binding site side-chain conformations are the failure mode, not backbone
enrichment_factor: ECR EF1%: PDB structures = 20.5 (avg); AF2 models = 8.8 (avg). PRC EF: PDB = 24.1; AF2 = 8.9. EF halved when using raw AF2 models.
pose_reproducibility: Y — PRC method enforces pose consensus across 4 programs
consensus_scoring: Y — ECR and PRC consensus scoring applied across AutoDock4/ICM/rDock/PLANTS
TF_specific: Y — nuclear receptors included: ANDR (androgen receptor); ESR1 (estrogen receptor α); PRGR (progesterone receptor) — all ligand-dependent TFs with defined LBD pocket
notes: CRITICAL WARNING FOR DAM-DRUG AF2 DOCKING PIPELINE: Raw AF2 models are UNSUITABLE for rigid HTD without binding-site refinement. EF1% halved (20.5→8.8) vs. PDB structures. High pLDDT does NOT guarantee docking suitability. MECHANISM: Side-chain conformations in binding site differ from holo crystal structures — even 0.16 Å backbone RMSD in ANDR caused EF to collapse from 9.0 to 0.0. NUCLEAR RECEPTOR / TF-SPECIFIC FINDING (HIGH RELEVANCE): AF2 models of ESR1/ANDR/PRGR predicted agonist-bound conformation — helix 12 pulled inward, closing the LBD. This state bias means AF2 models of TFs with LBDs (e.g. nuclear receptors) may mis-represent the druggable conformation. For TFs without LBD (IRF8/SPI1/RUNX1), the failure mode is side-chain disorder in the flat/shallow binding interface — even worse than LBD targets. MANDATORY PLAN AMENDMENT: Add AF2 model refinement step before docking (Rosetta Relax protocol / induced-fit docking / ensemble docking). Do NOT use raw AF2 output for AutoDock Vina screen. Add pLDDT ≥ 80 per-residue filter for binding site residues (binding-site pLDDT = 72 was the worst performer). Consensus scoring (≥3/4 tools agree on pose) partially mitigates AF2 side-chain noise — adopt PRC-equivalent protocol. MM-GBSA NOT performed in this study — cannot contribute ρ to meta-analysis.

docking_tool: DrugCLIP (contrastive learning — cosine similarity of pocket/ligand embeddings)
docking_scoring_function: Learned cosine similarity between 3D atomic pocket and ligand embeddings
target_class: GPCR / transporter / enzyme (no TF tested; no shallow PPI site benchmark)
target_name: 5HT2A receptor (5HT2AR); norepinephrine transporter (NET); TRIP12 (ubiquitin E3 ligase)
PDB_or_AF2_used: Both — head-to-head: holo PDB EF1%=29.31; AF2+GenPack EF1%=25.88; AF2+Fpocket EF1%=19.0 (GenPack mandatory for AF2)
binding_site_definition: GenPack (generative pocket refinement step) required for AF2 — Fpocket insufficient on raw AF2 models
virtual_screening_library_size: 500 million compounds (GenomeScreenDB precomputed); wet-lab screening N not specified
experimental_validation: Y — NET inhibitors confirmed by binding assay; 2 NET inhibitor crystal structures determined; TRIP12 inhibitors confirmed by biochemical assay
AF2_vs_PDB_comparison_performed: Y — explicit head-to-head: holo PDB EF1%=29.31; AF2+GenPack EF1%=25.88 (gap narrow); AF2+Fpocket EF1%=19.0
AF2_docking_RMSD_vs_crystal_Å: N/A — no RMSD reported (embedding method; no explicit pose prediction)
enrichment_factor: DUD-E EF1%=24.61; LIT-PCBA EF1%=5.36; AF2 EF1%=25.88 (DUD-E with GenPack)
ROC_AUC_docking: DUD-E AUC=77.42; BEDROC=39.86; LIT-PCBA AUC=59.54; BEDROC=7.29
TF_specific: N — no TF targets; no shallow PPI site benchmark — critical gap for DAM-DRUG TF targets
notes: POTENTIALLY PLAN-CHANGING: DrugCLIP runs LIT-PCBA in 38 seconds vs Glide-SP 3 days (128 CPUs). AF2 EF1%=25.88 vs holo=29.31 — gap is narrow (contrast: Scardino halved EF from 20.5→8.8). GenPack mandatory: raw Fpocket on AF2 gives EF1%=19.0 (drops below holo); GenPack restores to 25.88. CRITICAL CAVEAT: NO TF TARGETS TESTED — validated only on GPCR/transporter/enzyme with deep well-defined pockets. Performance on shallow TF binding sites (SPI1 ETS domain / IRF8 DBD / RUNX1-CBFB PPI) is UNKNOWN and likely lower. Training data (44K BioLiP2 complexes) likely contains few or no TF-small molecule pairs — potential domain gap. GenomeScreenDB covers ~10000 human proteins — check whether SPI1/IRF8/RUNX1/PPARG are precomputed (not confirmed in manuscript). PLAN ACTION: Add DrugCLIP as optional Phase 3 pre-screening filter for tractable targets (PPARG LBD / IKZF1 degrader); for flat TF sites (SPI1/IRF8/RUNX1) use full Vina/Gnina/AutoDock consensus pipeline. Open source: github.com/bowen-gao/DrugCLIP.

docking_tool: Glide (Schrödinger) with IFD-MD refinement (Induced Fit Docking + Molecular Dynamics — 5-model ensemble)
target_class: Kinases / nuclear receptors / proteases (37 DUD-E targets — no classical DBD-TFs)
target_name: 37 DUD-E targets including THRB (thyroid hormone receptor β) and GCR (glucocorticoid receptor) — ligand-dependent TFs with LBD
PDB_or_AF2_used: Both — head-to-head: apo PDB EF1%=11.4; raw AF2 EF1%=13.0; IFD-MD refined AF2 EF1%=18.9; holo PDB EF1%=24.2
virtual_screening_library_size: DUD-E (N varies by target — typically 1000–10000 compounds per target)
MD_simulation_performed: Y — as part of IFD-MD refinement workflow
MD_duration_ns: Not reported (IFD-MD default; not disclosed in ns units)
AF2_vs_PDB_comparison_performed: Y — apo PDB 11.4 / raw AF2 13.0 / IFD-MD AF2 18.9 / holo PDB 24.2
enrichment_factor: Raw AF2 EF1%=13.0; IFD-MD AF2 EF1%=18.9; apo PDB EF1%=11.4; holo PDB EF1%=24.2
pose_reproducibility: N — ensemble of 5 IFD-MD models (not multi-tool consensus)
consensus_scoring: Ensemble of 5 IFD-MD-refined models used (not multi-software consensus)
TF_specific: Partial — nuclear receptors THRB/GCR included (ligand-dependent TFs with LBD); no classical flat-site DBD TFs
evidence_tier: 2 (J Chem Inf Model 2023; retrospective benchmark; commercial Schrödinger tools)
notes: CONFIRMS REFINEMENT IS MANDATORY AND EFFECTIVE: IFD-MD closes 42% of the raw AF2→holo gap (13.0→18.9 of 11.1-point gap to holo 24.2). Raw AF2 EF1%=13.0 > Scardino 8.8 — reflects broader diverse target set. NUCLEAR RECEPTOR FAILURE CASE (RELEVANT TO PPARG): THRB AF2 structure had backbone misfolding (TRP504-THR510) — IFD-MD failed because it only refines active site side-chains not backbone errors. Lesson: if PPARG AF2 has backbone deviation in LBD helix-12 region check RMSD vs PDB first; backbone errors require homology modeling not IFD-MD. OPEN-SOURCE EQUIVALENT: Schrödinger IFD-MD is commercial; open-source equivalent = 50 ns explicit MD (GROMACS/AMBER) + Rosetta FastRelax — Wang 2019 supports 1-5 ns sufficiency; 50 ns is conservative and appropriate. RECOMMENDATION FOR DAM-DRUG: (1) Ensemble ≥5 AF2-refined models per target; (2) pilot screen with known actives before full production screen; (3) if AF2 backbone macrostate is wrong (e.g. LBD-TF in wrong helix-12 state) apply homology modeling first.

docking_tool: AutoDock Vina (primary docking); RF-Score / NNScore / RF-Score-VS / PLEC (ML rescoring)
docking_scoring_function: AutoDock Vina default (primary); RF-Score + NNScore consensus (best ML rescoring)
target_class: Bacterial enzymes — DNA replication / cell wall synthesis / transcription / metabolism
target_name: 296 E. coli essential proteins; 12 experimentally validated targets (gyrAB / dnaB / dnaG / murF / murA / murD / murC / ligA / rpoABCEZ / glmU / gmk)
PDB_or_AF2_used: AF2 primary; experimental PDB for 8 targets (head-to-head)
binding_site_definition: P2Rank binding site predictor applied to AF2 structures
virtual_screening_library_size: 39128 compounds (in-house HTS actives); 218 actives + 100 inactives for AUROC analysis
experimental_validation: Y — actives confirmed by in-house E. coli growth inhibition HTS; AUROC validated against confirmed actives/inactives
AF2_vs_PDB_comparison_performed: Y — experimental PDB AUROC=0.49 vs raw AF2 AUROC=0.46 (essentially identical — AF2 not the problem)
enrichment_factor: N/A — AUROC used as primary metric
ROC_AUC_docking: Raw AF2 AUROC=0.48 (12 targets avg); PDB AUROC=0.49 (8 targets); AF2+ML rescoring AUROC=0.63; best targets: gyrAB=0.71 / dnaB=0.65 / murF=0.63; worst: murC=0.18 / gmk=0.34
pose_reproducibility: N/A — AutoDock Vina does not enforce pose reproducibility
consensus_scoring: Partial — RF-Score + NNScore consensus gave best AUROC=0.63
TF_specific: N — rpoABCEZ (bacterial RNA polymerase) is nearest to TF context but is NOT a mammalian ETS/IRF/RUNX-family TF
evidence_tier: 2 (Mol Syst Biol 2022; experimental actives/inactives; bacterial context — limited generalizability to human TF/CNS targets)
notes: KEY MECHANISTIC INSIGHT: AUROC near-random (0.48) even on EXPERIMENTAL PDB structures (0.49). Conclusively demonstrates poor AUROC is a DOCKING ALGORITHM problem not an AF2 structure problem. IMPLICATION: Even with perfect TF structures (SPI1/IRF8/RUNX1 crystal structures from PDB) AutoDock Vina alone will rank actives poorly — ML rescoring or MM-GBSA is MANDATORY. ML RESCORING: RF-Score+NNScore consensus improved 0.48→0.63 using only open-source tools. PLAN ACTION: Add RF-Score or NNScore as ML rescoring step after Vina/Gnina (alongside MM-GBSA rescoring); treat raw Vina scores as insufficient ranking criterion. GENERALIZABILITY CAVEAT: All 12 validated targets are bacterial enzymes with deep active-site pockets; performance on shallow mammalian TF binding interfaces expected to be worse than AUROC=0.48 baseline. DAM-DRUG should expect AUROC=0.40-0.55 range for flat TF sites before rescoring.
